{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Manager (BDM)",
  "description": "Total Data Limited - Our client, a&nbsp;leading company in the Fintech space, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Business Development Manager (BDM)

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;
Employment type: Full-time
Industry: Financial Services

Job Description


	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Business Development Manager (BDM) to identify and develop import/export business clients with cross-border payment needs within the Nigerian market.
	This role focuses on high-value B2B client acquisition, relationship management, and business growth. It is not a retail or door-to-door sales role.


Job Responsibilities


	Identify and onboard importers and exporters with cross-border payment needs.
	Promote the company&rsquo;s local payment and foreign exchange solutions to corporate clients.
	Support clients with onboarding and usage of payment platforms and services.
	Conduct regular market visits, client meetings, and business association engagements.
	Build and maintain strong partnerships with freight forwarders, logistics companies, customs agents, and industry associations.
	Develop and maintain long-term relationships with clients to ensure continuous business growth.
	Gather market intelligence and provide feedback on client needs and market trends to management.
	Build a strong pipeline of import/export businesses across industries such as:
	
		Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG)
		Electronics
		Auto Parts
		Building Materials
		Textiles
		Machinery &amp; Equipment
	
	


Requirements


	Candidates should possess an OND, HND, or Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Finance, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years&rsquo; experience in Business Development or B2B Sales.
	Previous experience in Banking, Fintech, Cross-Border Services, Logistics, or related industries is highly preferred.
	Strong business development, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
	Highly motivated, target-driven, and able to work effectively in a fast-paced environment.
	Excellent communication and customer relationship management skills.
	An existing network within the importer/exporter community is an added advantage.
	Candidates with personal transportation are strongly preferred.


Location Preference:


	Candidates residing around Berger Auto Market, Mile 12, or nearby locations are preferred.


Compensation &amp; Benefits


	Monthly base salary of N250,000 / Month
	Attractive high commission structure
	Performance-based career growth opportunities
	Training and continuous support provided
	The more business you close, the higher your earnings potential.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-08",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-09",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Total Data Limited"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "198610"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Business Development Manager (BDM) Job at Total Data Limited




        
    

        
        Total Data Limited - Our client, a leading company in the Fintech space, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager (BDM)

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment type: Full-time
Industry: Financial Services

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Business Development Manager (BDM) to identify and develop import/export business clients with cross-border payment needs within the Nigerian market.
	This role focuses on high-value B2B client acquisition, relationship management, and business growth. It is not a retail or door-to-door sales role.


Job Responsibilities


	Identify and onboard importers and exporters with cross-border payment needs.
	Promote the company’s local payment and foreign exchange solutions to corporate clients.
	Support clients with onboarding and usage of payment platforms and services.
	Conduct regular market visits, client meetings, and business association engagements.
	Build and maintain strong partnerships with freight forwarders, logistics companies, customs agents, and industry associations.
	Develop and maintain long-term relationships with clients to ensure continuous business growth.
	Gather market intelligence and provide feedback on client needs and market trends to management.
	Build a strong pipeline of import/export businesses across industries such as:
	
		Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG)
		Electronics
		Auto Parts
		Building Materials
		Textiles
		Machinery & Equipment
	
	


Requirements


	Candidates should possess an OND, HND, or Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Finance, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years’ experience in Business Development or B2B Sales.
	Previous experience in Banking, Fintech, Cross-Border Services, Logistics, or related industries is highly preferred.
	Strong business development, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
	Highly motivated, target-driven, and able to work effectively in a fast-paced environment.
	Excellent communication and customer relationship management skills.
	An existing network within the importer/exporter community is an added advantage.
	Candidates with personal transportation are strongly preferred.


Location Preference:


	Candidates residing around Berger Auto Market, Mile 12, or nearby locations are preferred.


Compensation & Benefits


	Monthly base salary of N250,000 / Month
	Attractive high commission structure
	Performance-based career growth opportunities
	Training and continuous support provided
	The more business you close, the higher your earnings potential.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Executive",
  "description": "Cheaper Beta Enterprises is a fast-growing company with a focus on providing consumers with goods and services at the best market price. We are continually creating an atmosphere for employees to grow.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive
Job Location:&nbsp;Oniru, Lagos

Job Summary


	We are seeking a smart, result-driven, and highly motivated Business Development Executive to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for driving company growth, identifying new business opportunities, generating leads, and building strong client relationships.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify, qualify, and pursue new business opportunities through cold calling, networking, and market research.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with prospective and existing clients.
	Prepare and deliver compelling presentations, proposals, and contracts tailored to client needs.
	Monitor industry trends, competitor activities, and emerging market opportunities.
	Meet or exceed monthly, quarterly, and annual sales targets and KPIs.
	Collaborate with marketing, finance, and product teams to align strategies and ensure client satisfaction.
	Manage the sales process from prospecting to closing deals.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business, Marketing, Communications, or related field.
	1&ndash;3 years of proven experience in business development, sales, or a similar role.
	Strong communication, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
	Ability to analyze market trends, sales metrics, and forecast revenue.
	Proficiency in CRM tools (Salesforce, HubSpot, etc.) and Microsoft Office applications.
	Self-motivated, resilient, adaptable, and excellent at time management.


Skills Required:


	Excellent verbal and written communication
	Sales and negotiation skills
	Analytical and problem-solving ability
	Strong interpersonal


Skills:


	Goal-oriented mindset.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;info.cheaperbeta@gmail.com&nbsp;using &quot;Business Development Executive&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Business Development Executive Job at Cheaper Beta Enterprises




        
    

        
        Cheaper Beta Enterprises is a fast-growing company with a focus on providing consumers with goods and services at the best market price. We are continually creating an atmosphere for employees to grow.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive
Job Location: Oniru, Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a smart, result-driven, and highly motivated Business Development Executive to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for driving company growth, identifying new business opportunities, generating leads, and building strong client relationships.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify, qualify, and pursue new business opportunities through cold calling, networking, and market research.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with prospective and existing clients.
	Prepare and deliver compelling presentations, proposals, and contracts tailored to client needs.
	Monitor industry trends, competitor activities, and emerging market opportunities.
	Meet or exceed monthly, quarterly, and annual sales targets and KPIs.
	Collaborate with marketing, finance, and product teams to align strategies and ensure client satisfaction.
	Manage the sales process from prospecting to closing deals.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Marketing, Communications, or related field.
	1–3 years of proven experience in business development, sales, or a similar role.
	Strong communication, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
	Ability to analyze market trends, sales metrics, and forecast revenue.
	Proficiency in CRM tools (Salesforce, HubSpot, etc.) and Microsoft Office applications.
	Self-motivated, resilient, adaptable, and excellent at time management.


Skills Required:


	Excellent verbal and written communication
	Sales and negotiation skills
	Analytical and problem-solving ability
	Strong interpersonal


Skills:


	Goal-oriented mindset.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Business Development Executive" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Turbo Energy is a forward-thinking renewable energy and power solutions company committed to powering homes, businesses, and industries across Nigeria and beyond. We are driven by innovation, sustainability, and excellence.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Business Development Manager will drive revenue growth, strategic partnerships, and market expansion for the Payfor Power product portfolio.
	The role involves identifying new business opportunities, managing key accounts, developing go-to-market strategies, and building relationships with utilities, government agencies, real estate developers, commercial clients, telecom operators, and industrial customers.


Responsibilities
Business Development &amp; Sales:


	Identify and develop new business opportunities for Payfor Power solutions.
	Drive sales growth across residential, commercial, industrial, and utility sectors.
	Develop and execute strategic sales plans to achieve revenue targets.
	Build and maintain a strong sales pipeline and customer database.
	Lead proposal development, tender submissions, and contract negotiations.
	Conduct market intelligence and competitor analysis.


Client Relationship Management:
Build strong relationships with:


	Distribution Companies (DisCos)
	Government agencies
	Real estate developers
	Commercial and industrial customers
	Telecom operators
	EPC contractors
	Manage key accounts and ensure customer satisfaction and retention.
	Represent the company at industry events, exhibitions, and stakeholder meetings.


Partnership &amp; Market Expansion:


	Develop strategic partnerships with financiers, energy investors, OEMs, and project developers.
	Identify opportunities for mini-grid, embedded generation, solar hybrid, and smart metering projects.
	Support expansion into new geographic and sector markets.


Product &amp; Strategy Support:


	Work closely with technical and operations teams to align solutions with customer needs.
	Contribute to product positioning, pricing, and go-to-market strategies.
	Prepare business cases, forecasts, and performance reports for management.


Reporting &amp; Compliance:


	Maintain accurate sales reports and CRM updates.
	Achieve monthly and annual sales KPIs.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and industry regulations.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Business Development Manager Job at Turbo Energy Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Turbo Energy is a forward-thinking renewable energy and power solutions company committed to powering homes, businesses, and industries across Nigeria and beyond. We are driven by innovation, sustainability, and excellence.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Business Development Manager will drive revenue growth, strategic partnerships, and market expansion for the Payfor Power product portfolio.
	The role involves identifying new business opportunities, managing key accounts, developing go-to-market strategies, and building relationships with utilities, government agencies, real estate developers, commercial clients, telecom operators, and industrial customers.


Responsibilities
Business Development & Sales:


	Identify and develop new business opportunities for Payfor Power solutions.
	Drive sales growth across residential, commercial, industrial, and utility sectors.
	Develop and execute strategic sales plans to achieve revenue targets.
	Build and maintain a strong sales pipeline and customer database.
	Lead proposal development, tender submissions, and contract negotiations.
	Conduct market intelligence and competitor analysis.


Client Relationship Management:
Build strong relationships with:


	Distribution Companies (DisCos)
	Government agencies
	Real estate developers
	Commercial and industrial customers
	Telecom operators
	EPC contractors
	Manage key accounts and ensure customer satisfaction and retention.
	Represent the company at industry events, exhibitions, and stakeholder meetings.


Partnership & Market Expansion:


	Develop strategic partnerships with financiers, energy investors, OEMs, and project developers.
	Identify opportunities for mini-grid, embedded generation, solar hybrid, and smart metering projects.
	Support expansion into new geographic and sector markets.


Product & Strategy Support:


	Work closely with technical and operations teams to align solutions with customer needs.
	Contribute to product positioning, pricing, and go-to-market strategies.
	Prepare business cases, forecasts, and performance reports for management.


Reporting & Compliance:


	Maintain accurate sales reports and CRM updates.
	Achieve monthly and annual sales KPIs.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and industry regulations.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send CV to: [email protected] using the job title as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Facility & Logistics Manager (Events)",
  "description": "At Elvaridah, we emphasize on the critical matters and prospects of organizations such as business growth, strategy, planning, marketing, structure, and people management across different industries. We help companies focus on the growth of their businesses while aligning costs with strategy. With our all-inclusive approach and expertise, we capture values across the boundaries of any organization. Elvaridah focuses on optimizing all the different parts of a business as a unit.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Facility &amp; Logistics Manager (Events)&nbsp;&nbsp;
Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki Phase 1, Lagos

Job Summary


	We are seeking a hands-on, highly organised, and accountable Facility &amp; Logistics Manager to take full ownership of the company&#39;s physical assets &mdash; with a particular emphasis on furniture and event equipment, while simultaneously leading logistics operations for our rentals subsidiary.
	This role goes beyond paperwork and tracking systems.
	The ideal candidate will be deeply involved in the physical condition, presentation, and readiness of all furniture and event items, ensuring every piece leaving the warehouse is clean, functional, and event-ready.
	Alongside this, they will plan and execute seamless logistics for all bookings, managing drivers, handlers, and delivery schedules in a fast-paced events environment.
	The right person is detail-oriented, takes personal pride in the condition of every asset under their care, and leads by example on the floor as much as behind a desk.


Key Responsibilities
Furniture &amp; Asset Care (Core Focus):


	Physically inspect all furniture and event equipment before and after every event to ensure items are in excellent condition
	Identify, document, and immediately escalate any damage, wear, or deterioration of furniture, d&eacute;cor items, and equipment
	Coordinate prompt repairs, cleaning, reupholstering, or replacement of damaged or worn items
	Establish and enforce standards for how furniture is handled, stored, stacked, wrapped, and transported to minimise damage
	Ensure all items are properly cleaned, sanitised, and presented before deployment to any event
	Develop and maintain a preventive care schedule for high-use furniture and equipment
	Train and hold warehouse and logistics staff accountable for proper handling and care of all assets


Inventory &amp; Asset Management:


	Maintain a comprehensive, up-to-date inventory of all company assets including furniture, event equipment, tools, and vehicles
	Track asset movement across events and locations with accurate records at all times
	Ensure proper documentation, tagging, and accountability of every item
	Conduct regular audits and reconciliations to identify losses, discrepancies, or unaccounted items
	Develop and enforce systems to prevent asset loss, misuse, or unauthorised removal


Logistics &amp; Operations (Rentals Subsidiary):


	Plan and coordinate the transportation, delivery, setup support, and return of rental items for all bookings
	Oversee loading and offloading processes, ensuring furniture and equipment is handled carefully at every touchpoint
	Ensure timely dispatch and retrieval of all items, with zero tolerance for delays that affect event execution
	Manage logistics schedules and route planning for operational efficiency
	Supervise drivers, loaders, and handlers, setting clear expectations for handling standards
	Ensure compliance with safety and asset-handling protocols at all times


Fleet &amp; Vendor Coordination:


	Oversee company vehicles, including routine maintenance, documentation, and usage tracking
	Coordinate with external transporters, repair vendors, upholstery specialists, and suppliers as needed
	Negotiate logistics and maintenance costs while maintaining quality standards


Team Leadership &amp; Reporting:


	Lead and manage the warehouse, logistics, and asset handling teams day-to-day
	Provide regular reports on asset condition, utilisation, losses, damages, and logistics performance
	Implement process improvements to reduce asset damage rates, increase turnaround efficiency, and lower operational costs
	Foster a culture of care, accountability, and ownership across the team


Requirements


	3&ndash;5 years of experience in logistics, operations, or asset/inventory management , preferably within events, rentals, furniture, or hospitality
	Demonstrated hands-on experience caring for, maintaining, and managing physical furniture or event equipment
	Strong eye for condition, quality, and presentation of physical assets
	Experience managing vehicles, warehouse operations, and logistics teams
	Proven ability to lead, supervise, and hold teams accountable for handling standards
	Strong organisational skills with proficiency in record-keeping, Excel, or inventory management systems
	High level of personal accountability, integrity, and attention to detail
	Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple events simultaneously
	Good problem-solving and decision-making skills in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@elvaridah.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-08",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-08",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Elvaridah"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

  },

    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",

      "maxValue": 0,

    }

Facility & Logistics Manager (Events) Job at Elvaridah




        
    

        
        At Elvaridah, we emphasize on the critical matters and prospects of organizations such as business growth, strategy, planning, marketing, structure, and people management across different industries. We help companies focus on the growth of their businesses while aligning costs with strategy. With our all-inclusive approach and expertise, we capture values across the boundaries of any organization. Elvaridah focuses on optimizing all the different parts of a business as a unit.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Facility & Logistics Manager (Events)  
Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a hands-on, highly organised, and accountable Facility & Logistics Manager to take full ownership of the company's physical assets — with a particular emphasis on furniture and event equipment, while simultaneously leading logistics operations for our rentals subsidiary.
	This role goes beyond paperwork and tracking systems.
	The ideal candidate will be deeply involved in the physical condition, presentation, and readiness of all furniture and event items, ensuring every piece leaving the warehouse is clean, functional, and event-ready.
	Alongside this, they will plan and execute seamless logistics for all bookings, managing drivers, handlers, and delivery schedules in a fast-paced events environment.
	The right person is detail-oriented, takes personal pride in the condition of every asset under their care, and leads by example on the floor as much as behind a desk.


Key Responsibilities
Furniture & Asset Care (Core Focus):


	Physically inspect all furniture and event equipment before and after every event to ensure items are in excellent condition
	Identify, document, and immediately escalate any damage, wear, or deterioration of furniture, décor items, and equipment
	Coordinate prompt repairs, cleaning, reupholstering, or replacement of damaged or worn items
	Establish and enforce standards for how furniture is handled, stored, stacked, wrapped, and transported to minimise damage
	Ensure all items are properly cleaned, sanitised, and presented before deployment to any event
	Develop and maintain a preventive care schedule for high-use furniture and equipment
	Train and hold warehouse and logistics staff accountable for proper handling and care of all assets


Inventory & Asset Management:


	Maintain a comprehensive, up-to-date inventory of all company assets including furniture, event equipment, tools, and vehicles
	Track asset movement across events and locations with accurate records at all times
	Ensure proper documentation, tagging, and accountability of every item
	Conduct regular audits and reconciliations to identify losses, discrepancies, or unaccounted items
	Develop and enforce systems to prevent asset loss, misuse, or unauthorised removal


Logistics & Operations (Rentals Subsidiary):


	Plan and coordinate the transportation, delivery, setup support, and return of rental items for all bookings
	Oversee loading and offloading processes, ensuring furniture and equipment is handled carefully at every touchpoint
	Ensure timely dispatch and retrieval of all items, with zero tolerance for delays that affect event execution
	Manage logistics schedules and route planning for operational efficiency
	Supervise drivers, loaders, and handlers, setting clear expectations for handling standards
	Ensure compliance with safety and asset-handling protocols at all times


Fleet & Vendor Coordination:


	Oversee company vehicles, including routine maintenance, documentation, and usage tracking
	Coordinate with external transporters, repair vendors, upholstery specialists, and suppliers as needed
	Negotiate logistics and maintenance costs while maintaining quality standards


Team Leadership & Reporting:


	Lead and manage the warehouse, logistics, and asset handling teams day-to-day
	Provide regular reports on asset condition, utilisation, losses, damages, and logistics performance
	Implement process improvements to reduce asset damage rates, increase turnaround efficiency, and lower operational costs
	Foster a culture of care, accountability, and ownership across the team


Requirements


	3–5 years of experience in logistics, operations, or asset/inventory management , preferably within events, rentals, furniture, or hospitality
	Demonstrated hands-on experience caring for, maintaining, and managing physical furniture or event equipment
	Strong eye for condition, quality, and presentation of physical assets
	Experience managing vehicles, warehouse operations, and logistics teams
	Proven ability to lead, supervise, and hold teams accountable for handling standards
	Strong organisational skills with proficiency in record-keeping, Excel, or inventory management systems
	High level of personal accountability, integrity, and attention to detail
	Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple events simultaneously
	Good problem-solving and decision-making skills in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Staff Accountant Job at EMADOR Nigeria Integrated Resources




        
    

        
        EMADOR Nigeria Integrated Resources is a dynamic, multi-sector business dedicated to delivering innovative and reliable solutions across the energy, real estate, and general trading industries. The company specializes in solar energy sales and services, providing sustainable and cost-effective power solutions to homes and businesses.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Staff Accountant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	We are looking for a Staff Accountant to manage general accounting and financial processes of our company.


Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Maintain and update accounting records and files
	Analyze budgets and create expense reports
	Examine tax policies and handle tax payments and returns
	Meet with clients to discuss confidential accounting issues
	Post transactions and categorize records in the general ledger (e.g. by assets, liabilities and expenses)
	Reconcile bank statements
	Analyze transactions with internal and external stakeholders
	Conduct month-end and year-end closures
	Prepare documents for audits
	Advise clients on financial issues via email or phone
	Apply new accounting policies and ensure compliance with rules and regulations
	Report to the Accounting Manager and work to improve financial processes.


Requirements


	Excellent use of Microsoft Office Word and Excel; very good with spreadsheets
	Knowledge of an accounting information system is a plus
	Good math skills
	Attention to detail
	Strong organizational and communication skills
	Confidentiality and integrity.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
